title: Lowest common ancestor of two keys in a binary search tree

informal_statement.text:
Given a binary search tree whose in-order traversal is strictly ascending and two keys both present in the tree, return the key of the lowest node that has both keys in its subtree.

[Grid variant 001 — baseline authoritative informal contract for this algorithm family. Same reference implementation and harness as the rest of the family grid; used for parity and verbatim drift checks across releases.]
preconditions: the input tree is a valid BST
both query keys are present in the tree
required_properties: the returned key appears in the tree
both query keys are in the subtree rooted at the returned key
no proper descendant of the returned key has both query keys in its subtree
edge_cases: both keys equal (returned key equals them)
one key is an ancestor of the other
keys on opposite sides of the root
tree with only a single path
